concepts.label: Cryptocurrency
concepts.reason: The header introduces the main topic of the paragraph: cryptocurrencies.

paragraphs.text: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. The whitepaper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System" introduced a decentralized digital currency that operates without central banks or intermediaries.

concepts.label: Bitcoin as a decentralized digital currency without intermediaries

concepts.reason: This sentence gives an explicit explanation of what Bitcoin is and how it operates.

concepts.label: Blockchain technology
concepts.reason: The header introduces the main domain-specific topic of the section.

paragraphs.text: A blockchain is a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers. Each block contains a cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating an immutable chain. Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second, while Visa handles around 24,000.

concepts.label: Bitcoin vs Visa transaction throughput
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin blockchain roughly 7 transactions per second Visa around 24000 transactions per second
concepts.reason: This is a quantitative comparison of processing capacity that benefits from verification.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second

concepts.label: Blocks linked by cryptographic hashes

concepts.reason: This is a technical claim about how blockchain blocks are connected and why the chain is considered immutable.
surfaceForms: cryptographic hash of the previous block

concepts.label: Blockchain
concepts.reason: The paragraph explicitly defines blockchain as a type of ledger system.
surfaceForms: distributed ledger that records all transactions

paragraphs.text: The consensus mechanism Bitcoin uses is called Proof of Work, where miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles. The first miner to solve the puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward. This reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years.

concepts.label: Bitcoin block reward halving schedule
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin block reward started at 50 BTC halves approximately every four years
concepts.reason: This is a specific factual claim about Bitcoin's initial issuance and halving cadence.

concepts.label: Bitcoin miner block reward process
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin first miner to solve puzzle adds next block receives newly minted bitcoin reward
concepts.reason: This describes the core procedure by which a miner wins the right to append a block and obtain the reward.
surfaceForms: The first miner to solve the puzzle
receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward

concepts.reason: The paragraph explicitly explains Bitcoin's consensus mechanism and how it works at a high level.

paragraphs.text: Ethereum was proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015. It introduced smart contracts—self-executing programs stored on the blockchain that automatically enforce agreements when conditions are met.

concepts.label: Smart contracts are self-executing blockchain programs that enforce agreements
concepts.searchQuery: smart contracts self-executing programs stored on the blockchain definition
concepts.reason: Explicit definition of a key blockchain concept introduced in the paragraph.
surfaceForms: self-executing programs stored on the blockchain
automatically enforce agreements when conditions are met

paragraphs.text: In September 2022, Ethereum transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake in an event called "The Merge." This reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation.

paragraphs.text: DeFi applications allow users to lend, borrow, and trade without traditional financial intermediaries. The total value locked in DeFi protocols peaked at over $180 billion in late 2021 before declining significantly.

concepts.label: Regulatory landscape
concepts.reason: The header introduces the topic area of regulation and policy context, which is a meaningful domain term for the section.

paragraphs.text: The SEC has classified most cryptocurrencies as securities, with notable exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum. The landmark SEC v. Ripple case in 2023 established that programmatic sales of tokens on exchanges do not necessarily constitute securities transactions.

concepts.label: Bitcoin and Ethereum as exceptions to SEC securities classification
concepts.searchQuery: SEC exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum securities classification
concepts.reason: The paragraph identifies specific exceptions to the SEC's classification approach, which is a verifiable regulatory claim.
